title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At the gravesite of Patrick and Frances Ives, Poirot cleans footprints off the tombstone and reflects on the couple's desire to rest together. This reflection prompts Carterpillar to contemplate the relationship between Patrick and Nancy Duane. Carterpillar shares a sudden idea with Poirot, which Poirot enthusiastically validates, claiming it makes all the pieces of the case fall into place. Poirot then instructs Carterpillar to return to London to gather the personal belongings and luggage of Harry Westmacott and Adele Grant, noting that Mr. Nigges's belongings were taken by his brother, Richard Nigges.

The following day at 4:15 pm, Poirot and Carterpillar attend a gathering at the Blore House Hotel restaurant to reveal the truth. The staff, including John Good, Thomas Brien, and Raphael Pabk, are present. Lazarus is seen interacting with Inspector Stanley Bill, referring to the situation as the "alphabet cufflinks murder case." Various guests arrive, including Samuel Kid with Jenny Hobbs, and Henry Nigges. Nancy Duane arrives accompanied by Sir John Wallace, Lady Wallace, and their maid, Dorkas; she wears a scarf to hide her red eyes.

During the gathering, Carterpillar observes Thomas Brine holding hands with a black-haired woman, which Poirot notes is a different woman from the blonde in the brown coat who previously kissed Brine in the garden. Carterpillar also spots Phyllis Spence, a waitress from the "Jolly Coffee Shop," disguised in a large hat at the back of the room. Poirot silences the crowd and prepares to reveal the truth of the investigation.
